homeIntro.heading: Why you need a privacy policy
homeIntro.body: Every website, SaaS app, or e-commerce store that collects personal data needs a privacy policy. It's required by law under GDPR, CCPA, and dozens of other regulations worldwide. But hiring a lawyer costs $500+ per draft, and generic templates don't account for your specific business practices — the services you use, the data you collect, and the jurisdictions you operate in.
homeIntro.body2: PrivacyPolGen solves this. My intelligent wizard asks you 5 targeted questions about your business, walks you through the data you collect and the third-party services you integrate, then generates a complete, customized privacy policy in under 60 seconds. All of it happens in your browser — nothing is sent to a server.

features.label: Features
features.heading: Everything you need. Nothing you don't.
features.subtext: A privacy policy generator purpose-built for indie developers, SaaS founders, and small business owners who need a real, compliant policy without spending $500+ on a lawyer or struggling with generic one-size-fits-all templates.

laws.title: Laws covered out of the box
laws.body: GDPR, CCPA/CPRA, CalOPPA, COPPA, PIPEDA, LGPD, and DPDP Act. Select the ones that apply to your business and I tailor the clauses accordingly.

stack.title: Tailored to your stack
stack.body: Pick from 25+ popular third-party services — Stripe, Google Analytics, Plausible, Mailchimp, SendGrid, and more — and I add the right contractual disclosures for each one automatically.

url.title: Shareable public URL
url.body: Every policy gets a unique shareable URL encoded entirely in your browser. Host it on your own domain, embed it in your site, or send the link to your lawyer for review.

export.title: Export anywhere
export.body: Copy as Markdown, HTML, or plain text with one click. The Premium tier adds PDF and DOCX export for polished documentation.

legal.title: Real legal text, not Lorem Ipsum
legal.body: Every clause is hand-written by legal professionals who reference the actual sections of GDPR, CCPA, COPPA, PIPEDA, LGPD, and DPDP Act they satisfy.

privacy.title: Zero data, zero tracking
privacy.body: Your answers never leave your device. They're compressed and encoded into the URL itself using client-side DEFLATE. I don't run a backend, I don't log IPs, and I never see your policy content.

business.title: Tell me about your business
business.body: Tell me your business name, website URL, business type, and primary jurisdiction. I pre-fill smart defaults for the most common cases.

data.title: Pick what you collect
data.body: Check off the kinds of data your business collects — names, email addresses, payment info, usage data, cookies, and more. I've pre-selected common items based on your industry.

services.title: List your services
services.body: Select from 25+ third-party services like Stripe, Google Analytics, Mailchimp, Plausible, and SendGrid. Each selection adds the appropriate contractual disclosures.

laws.title: Choose applicable laws
laws.body: Choose from GDPR, CCPA/CPRA, CalOPPA, COPPA, PIPEDA, LGPD, DPDP Act, and PDPA-SG. I default to the strictest common case so you're covered everywhere.

policy.title: Get your policy
policy.body: Get your complete privacy policy as Markdown, HTML, or plain text. Share it via a public URL that stays with you, or copy-paste directly into your website footer.

items.a: A privacy policy is a legal document that explains how a website, SaaS app, e-commerce store, or mobile application collects, uses, stores, and shares personal information from its users. It covers what data is collected (names, emails, payment info, cookies, etc.), why it's collected, who it's shared with, and what rights users have over their data. Privacy policies are required by laws such as GDPR (EU), CCPA (California), CalOPPA, and many others worldwide. Every website or app that collects personal data must have a privacy policy.

items.q: Why is a privacy policy important?
items.a: A privacy policy is important for three reasons: (1) Legal compliance — laws like GDPR, CCPA, and CalOPPA require one; non-compliance can result in fines of up to 4% of global annual revenue (GDPR) or $2,500 per violation (CCPA). (2) User trust — 84% of consumers say transparency about data practices influences their decision to engage with a business. (3) Third-party requirements — services like Google Analytics, Stripe, and the Apple App Store require you to have a privacy policy. Without one, you risk legal penalties, lost customer confidence, and being blocked from essential platforms.

items.q: Do I need a privacy policy on my website?
items.a: Yes, if your website collects any form of personal data — contact form entries, email newsletter signups, analytics cookies, user accounts, or payment information — you need a privacy policy. It's required by law under GDPR (EU/EEA/UK), CCPA/CPRA (California), CalOPPA (California), PIPEDA (Canada), LGPD (Brazil), and many others. Even if you don't operate in those jurisdictions, if your website is accessible to users there (which most websites are), the laws apply. The only websites that don't need a privacy policy are those that collect absolutely no personal data — no contact forms, no analytics, no cookies, no newsletter.

items.q: What laws require a privacy policy?
items.a: The most notable laws that require a privacy policy include: GDPR (EU/EEA/UK) — requires a clear, accessible privacy notice for any personal data processing; CCPA/CPRA (California) — mandates disclosure of data collection, sharing, and consumer rights; CalOPPA (California) — requires commercial websites to post a conspicuous privacy policy; COPPA (US) — if you collect data from children under 13; PIPEDA (Canada); LGPD (Brazil); and DPDP Act (India). Because these laws have overlapping requirements, a well-written privacy policy that covers the strictest common case (e.g., GDPR-level disclosure) is the safest approach.

items.q: How to write a privacy policy?
items.a: Writing a privacy policy involves three steps. First, audit what personal data your business collects (names, emails, payment info, cookies, analytics, etc.) and who you share it with (payment processors, analytics tools, email services). Second, identify the privacy laws that apply to you based on where your users are located (GDPR for EU, CCPA for California, etc.). Third, draft clauses covering: what data you collect, how you use it, cookies and tracking, third-party disclosures, data retention, security practices, user rights, and contact information. The easiest way is to use PrivacyPolGen — the free wizard asks 5 questions about your business and generates a complete, lawyer-grade privacy policy in under 60 seconds.

items.q: How to write a privacy policy for an app?
items.a: Writing a privacy policy for a mobile app follows the same principles as a website, with additional disclosures required: device identifiers (IDFA, AAID), permissions (camera, microphone, location, contacts), SDK data (from ad or analytics SDKs), and push notifications. Both the Apple App Store and Google Play Store mandate a privacy policy for any app that collects data. Include sections about mobile-specific data types and the third-party SDKs your app uses. You can use PrivacyPolGen to generate an app-specific policy by selecting "Mobile App" as your business type in the wizard.

items.q: How to create a privacy policy for free?
items.a: You can create a complete, legally compliant privacy policy for free using PrivacyPolGen. Answer 5 quick questions about your business, the data you collect, the third-party services you use, and the laws that apply to you. The wizard generates a fully customized policy in under 60 seconds — no account, no credit card, no data sent to a server. You can copy it as Markdown, HTML, or plain text, or share it via a public URL. The core generator is and always will be free.

items.q: Is this privacy policy generator really free?
items.a: Yes. You can generate, copy, and host the resulting privacy policy completely free of charge. The core generator will always be free. I have a Premium tier planned with PDF/DOCX exports and multi-page policies, but the core generator will always be free.

items.q: Does this cover GDPR, CCPA, and other laws?
items.a: Yes. Select the laws that apply to your business in Step 4 of the wizard, and I'll include the appropriate clauses (GDPR legal basis, CCPA opt-out, COPPA parental consent, PIPEDA access rights, LGPD legal basis, DPDP Act grievance officer, PDPA-SG consent mechanisms).

items.q: Do I need an account to generate a privacy policy?
items.a: No account required. Just answer the questions in the wizard and the policy will be generated. Your answers are encoded into the shareable URL itself — nothing is sent to my servers.

items.q: How do I add the policy to my website?
items.a: Create a page on your site (e.g., /privacy), paste the HTML output, and link to it from your footer. If you want a hosted version, share the generated /p?h=... URL — it works as a standalone public page.

items.q: Can I edit the policy after generating it?
items.a: Yes. Just edit the markdown or HTML in any text editor and host the file on your own website. The output is yours to modify freely. The Premium tier will offer editable in-app editing with version history.

items.q: Does the policy include a cookie section?
items.a: Yes — if you select 'Cookies' in Step 2 of the wizard, I include a full cookies section describing essential, analytics, preference, and marketing cookies. I also list which third-party cookies each service you integrate may set.

Nowhere. The site uses client-side compression (DEFLATE + Base64 URL-safe encoding) to pack your answers into the URL hash. When you share the URL, the recipient's browser decodes the policy locally. I do not run a backend, I do not log wizard answers, and I do not have access to your policy content.

items.q: Is the generated policy legally binding?
items.a: The generated policy is a starting template that covers the most common legal requirements (GDPR, CCPA/CPRA, CalOPPA, COPPA, PIPEDA, LGPD, DPDP Act, PDPA-SG). However, every business is different. I strongly recommend having a lawyer review the final policy before relying on it, especially if you operate in regulated industries (health, finance, children's services).

items.q: Will my policy update if laws change?
items.a: The policy you generate is a static document at a specific point in time. If privacy laws change, you'll need to regenerate it and update the version on your site. I update my clause library continuously, and the wizard is always in sync with current best practices.

items.q: What if I don't know what laws apply to me?
items.a: If you are unsure which privacy laws apply to your business, I recommend leaving the default selections (GDPR, CCPA/CPRA, and CalOPPA) as a starting point. If you're unsure, leaving the defaults selected is usually the safest choice — covering the most laws never hurts, and the policy will still be valid in jurisdictions where the law doesn't apply.

items.q: What's the difference between the free and Premium tiers?
items.a: Free gives you a complete privacy policy in markdown, HTML, and plain text — copy/paste into your site. Premium adds PDF and DOCX export, cookie banner code generation, custom branding, and hosted policy pages with version history.

items.q: Can I use this for a client project?
items.a: Absolutely. You can generate policies for unlimited client projects on the free tier. I just ask that you don't redistribute the generator itself or resell the output unmodified as a competing service.

intro.title: 1. Acceptance of Terms
intro.body: By accessing or using PrivacyPolGen (the "Service"), operated by Reuben Richard Lancer ("I," "me," or "my"), you agree to be bound by these Terms of Service. If you do not agree to all of these terms, do not use the Service.

description.title: 2. Description of Service
description.body: PrivacyPolGen is a free, client-side privacy policy generator. I provide templated legal language based on your inputs to help you create a starting-point privacy policy for your website, application, or business. The Service runs entirely in your browser — no data you enter is transmitted to, stored on, or processed by my servers.

notLegalAdvice.title: 3. Not Legal Advice
notLegalAdvice.body: The content generated by PrivacyPolGen is for informational purposes only and does not constitute legal advice. I am not a law firm, I am not your attorney, and the use of this Service does not create an attorney-client relationship. You should not act or refrain from acting based on any content generated by the Service without first consulting a qualified attorney licensed in your jurisdiction. The generated policy is a starting template and may not address all legal requirements applicable to your specific situation, business model, or industry.

noWarranties.title: 4. Disclaimer of Warranties
noWarranties.body: THE SERVICE IS PROVIDED "AS IS" AND "AS AVAILABLE," WITHOUT WARRANTY OF ANY KIND, EXPRESS OR IMPLIED. TO THE FULLEST EXTENT PERMITTED BY APPLICABLE LAW, WE DISCLAIM ALL WARRANTIES, WHETHER EXPRESS, IMPLIED, STATUTORY, OR OTHERWISE, INCLUDING BUT NOT LIMITED TO ANY WARRANTIES OF MERCHANTABILITY, FITNESS FOR A PARTICULAR PURPOSE, TITLE, NON-INFRINGEMENT, AND ACCURACY. WE DO NOT WARRANT THAT THE SERVICE WILL BE UNINTERRUPTED, ERROR-FREE, SECURE, OR THAT ANY GENERATED CONTENT WILL BE COMPLETE, ACCURATE, OR LEGALLY SUFFICIENT FOR YOUR NEEDS.

limitationLiability.title: 5. Limitation of Liability
limitationLiability.body: TO THE MAXIMUM EXTENT PERMITTED BY APPLICABLE LAW, IN NO EVENT SHALL I BE LIABLE FOR ANY INDIRECT, INCIDENTAL, SPECIAL, CONSEQUENTIAL, OR PUNITIVE DAMAGES, INCLUDING BUT NOT LIMITED TO LOSS OF PROFITS, DATA, USE, GOODWILL, OR OTHER INTANGIBLE LOSSES, ARISING OUT OF OR RELATING TO YOUR USE OF, OR INABILITY TO USE, THE SERVICE — INCLUDING BUT NOT LIMITED TO ANY FINES, PENALTIES, OR LEGAL LIABILITIES INCURRED AS A RESULT OF USING A GENERATED POLICY. MY TOTAL LIABILITY TO YOU FOR ALL CLAIMS ARISING FROM OR RELATING TO THESE TERMS OR THE SERVICE SHALL NOT EXCEED THE GREATER OF $1.00 OR THE AMOUNT YOU HAVE PAID ME IN THE PRECEDING TWELVE MONTHS.

indemnification.title: 6. Indemnification
indemnification.body: You agree to defend, indemnify, and hold me harmless from and against any claims, liabilities, damages, judgments, awards, losses, costs, expenses, or fees (including reasonable attorneys' fees) arising out of or relating to your use of the Service, the privacy policy you generate, or your violation of these Terms.

intellectualProperty.title: 7. Intellectual Property
intellectualProperty.body: The Service, including its text, templates, clauses, and structure, is owned by me and protected by copyright and other intellectual property laws. The privacy policy you generate is yours to use, modify, and distribute for any lawful purpose. However, you may not copy, redistribute, or resell the generator itself or the underlying clause templates as a competing service.

thirdParty.title: 8. Third-Party Services
thirdParty.body: The Service may display references to third-party services (e.g., analytics, payment processors). These references are provided for informational purposes only. I am not responsible for the content, privacy practices, or terms of any third-party services referenced in generated policies.

changes.title: 9. Changes to Terms
changes.body: I reserve the right to modify these Terms at any time. Changes will be posted on this page with an updated "Last updated" date. Your continued use of the Service after any changes constitutes your acceptance of the new Terms. I encourage you to review these Terms periodically.

governingLaw.title: 10. Governing Law
governingLaw.body: These Terms shall be governed by and construed in accordance with the laws of the State of Delaware, United States, without regard to its conflict of laws principles. Any disputes arising under these Terms shall be resolved exclusively in the courts of the State of Delaware. Nothing in this section limits your rights under applicable consumer protection laws in your country or state of residence. If you are a resident of a jurisdiction with mandatory consumer protections (including but not limited to the European Union and the United Kingdom), those protections remain unaffected.

contact.title: 11. Contact
contact.body: If you have any questions about these Terms, please contact me at benatwork4747@gmail.com.

intro.title: Introduction
intro.body: PrivacyPolGen ("I," "me," or "my") operates the website at privacypolgen.com (the "Service"). This page explains my approach to privacy — which, in short, is that I collect almost nothing.
intro.body2: Unlike most online services, PrivacyPolGen does not have a backend server. There is no database, no user accounts, no analytics tracking, and no data storage on my side. The privacy policy generator runs entirely in your browser.

noCollection.title: Information I Do NOT Collect
noCollection.body: I do not collect, store, or process any personal information about you. Specifically:
list: No account data: I do not require or support user accounts.
No personal information: I do not ask for or collect your name, email address, or any other personal identifier.
No payment information: The Service is free. I do not process payments.
No analytics: I do not run any analytics, tracking pixels, or telemetry on my site.
No cookies for tracking: I do not use cookies for analytics, advertising, or any tracking purposes.
No policy data: The answers you provide in the generator wizard are encoded directly into the URL. They are never sent to, stored on, or processed by any server.

technical.title: Technical Data
technical.body: My hosting provider may automatically collect standard server log data such as your IP address, browser type, and requested pages. This data is used solely for the operational purposes of serving the website and preventing abuse. I do not access, analyze, or share this data.

localStorage.title: Local Storage
localStorage.body: I use your browser's localStorage to remember two preferences: your selected theme (light/dark) and your language choice. This data stays on your device. I cannot access it, and it is never transmitted anywhere. You can clear these at any time via your browser settings.

thirdParty.title: Third-Party Services
thirdParty.body: I use the following third-party services, which have their own privacy practices:
list: Fonts (Google Fonts): Font files are loaded from Google's servers. Google may collect information about font requests per its privacy policy. I self-host where possible to minimize this.
Hosting: My static site is served via my hosting provider. Standard server logs may be retained temporarily for security and operational purposes.

dataRetention.title: Data Retention
dataRetention.body: Since I collect no personal data, there is nothing to retain. Any server logs kept by my hosting provider are subject to their retention policies and are not accessed by me.

yourRights.title: Your Rights
yourRights.body: Because I collect no personal data, there is no personal data for me to provide, correct, or delete. If you have concerns about data that may be inadvertently captured in server logs by my hosting provider, please contact me and I will investigate in good faith.

changes.title: Changes to This Policy
changes.body: If I change this privacy policy, I will update the "Last updated" date at the top. Since I collect no data, changes are likely to be rare and minor.

contact.title: Contact
contact.body: If you have questions about this privacy policy, please contact me at benatwork4747@gmail.com.

aboutPage.heading: About PrivacyPolGen
aboutPage.subtext: A simple, free privacy policy generator built for indie developers — not for big law firms.

mission.heading: My Mission
mission.body: Privacy policies shouldn't cost $500 or require a lawyer. I built PrivacyPolGen to give indie developers, SaaS founders, and small business owners a fast, free way to generate accurate privacy policies that comply with global regulations like GDPR, CCPA, and more.
mission.body2: Everything runs in your browser — your data never touches a server. No accounts, no tracking, no hidden fees.

whyFree.body: I believe privacy compliance should be accessible to everyone. The core generator will always be free. I may introduce optional premium features (like PDF export) in the future, but the generator itself stays free — no signup, no limits, no catch.

preview.collectSentence: We collect {types} to provide our services.

dataTypes.payments: Payment information (processed by our payment provider, not stored on our servers)

google-analytics.name: Google Analytics
google-analytics.description: Web traffic and user behavior analytics provided by Google.

plausible.name: Plausible Analytics
plausible.description: Privacy-friendly analytics that don't use cookies or collect personal data.

mixpanel.name: Mixpanel
mixpanel.description: Product analytics for tracking user actions within web and mobile apps.

stripe.name: Stripe
stripe.description: Payment processing for credit card transactions.

paypal.name: PayPal
paypal.description: Online payment processor supporting PayPal balance and linked accounts.

lemonsqueezy.name: LemonSqueezy
lemonsqueezy.description: Merchant of record for digital product sales and subscriptions.

mailchimp.name: Mailchimp
mailchimp.description: Email newsletter delivery and audience management.

convertkit.name: ConvertKit
convertkit.description: Email marketing platform for creators and small businesses.

facebook-pixel.name: Facebook Pixel
facebook-pixel.description: Conversion tracking and audience-building for Facebook and Instagram ads.

google-oauth.name: Google Sign-In
google-oauth.description: OAuth login using a Google account.

facebook-login.name: Facebook Login
facebook-login.description: OAuth login using a Facebook account.

youtube-embeds.name: YouTube Embeds
youtube-embeds.description: Embedded YouTube videos on our pages.

vimeo-embeds.name: Vimeo Embeds
vimeo-embeds.description: Embedded Vimeo videos on our pages.

cloudflare.name: Cloudflare
cloudflare.description: CDN, DDoS protection, and DNS services.

gdpr.name: GDPR
gdpr.description: EU/EEA/UK comprehensive data protection law. Requires explicit consent, data subject rights, and lawful basis for processing.

ccpa.name: CCPA/CPRA
ccpa.fullName: California Consumer Privacy Act / California Privacy Rights Act
ccpa.description: California state privacy law. Grants California residents rights over their personal information, including the right to know, delete, and opt out of sale.

caloppa.name: CalOPPA
caloppa.description: Requires commercial websites that collect personal information from California users to post a conspicuous privacy policy.

coppa.name: COPPA
coppa.description: Federal law governing the online collection of personal information from children under 13.

pipeda.name: PIPEDA
pipeda.fullName: Personal Information Protection and Electronic Documents Act
pipeda.description: Canadian federal privacy law governing the collection, use, and disclosure of personal information in commercial activities.

lgpd.name: LGPD
lgpd.fullName: Lei Geral de Proteção de Dados
lgpd.description: Brazil's comprehensive data protection law, modeled after the GDPR.

dpdp.name: DPDP Act
dpdp.description: India's comprehensive data protection law governing the processing of digital personal data, effective 2025.

personal-blog.name: Personal Blog
personal-blog.description: A personal blog or content site. Typically collects emails via newsletter and uses basic analytics.

saas.name: SaaS Application
saas.description: Software-as-a-service web application with user accounts, payments, and integrations.

ecommerce.name: E-commerce Store
ecommerce.description: Online store selling physical or digital products with shopping cart and checkout.

mobile-app.name: Mobile App
mobile-app.description: iOS or Android app with in-app accounts, analytics, and possibly push notifications.

portfolio.name: Portfolio Website
portfolio.description: Personal portfolio or agency site showcasing work. Minimal data collection.

agency.name: Agency / Studio
agency.description: Marketing, design, or development agency with client work and contact forms.

policyReader.errorNoHash: No policy hash found in the URL. Generate a policy first.
policyReader.errorInvalid: This policy link is invalid or corrupted. Please generate a new one.
policyReader.title: {{businessName}} — Privacy Policy

cookieReader.errorNoHash: No cookie policy hash found in the URL. Generate a cookie policy first.
cookieReader.errorInvalid: This cookie policy link is invalid or corrupted. Please generate a new one.
cookieReader.title: Cookie Policy — {{businessName}}
cookieReader.description: Generate a compliant cookie policy for your website. Customize disclosures about cookies, tracking technologies, and user consent choices.
cookieReader.pageSubtext: Review your cookie policy. It details the cookies and tracking technologies used on this website and your rights regarding them.

termsReader.errorNoHash: No terms hash found in the URL. Generate terms first.
termsReader.errorInvalid: This terms link is invalid or corrupted. Please generate a new one.
termsReader.title: Terms of Service — {{businessName}}
termsReader.description: Generate terms and conditions for your website, SaaS, or e-commerce store. Customize rules for accounts, UGC, payments, and more.
termsReader.pageSubtext: Review the terms and conditions that govern the use of this website, including legal disclaimers, liabilities, and user rights.

gdpr.description: Complete templates and best practices for European data protection.

ccpa.description: California privacy requirements and implementation strategies.

cta.subtext: Generate a compliant privacy policy in under 60 seconds using my intelligent generator. No legal expertise required.
